meaning: n.扑动,闪烁,闪光,颤动vi.闪动,闪烁,摇动,扑动翅膀,[美俚]昏倒,假装昏倒vt.使摇曳,使闪烁
examples: Looking through the window I saw the flicker of flames.
A flicker of a smile crossed her face.
Watch the flames flicker in the room.
The lights of another fishing boat flicker in the darkness away to the right.
There's hype, there's bluster, there's exaggeration and the unmistakable flicker of a new urban legend.
